name: OpenCost
description: Real-time cost monitoring for Kubernetes workloads — measures and allocates spend by namespace, deployment, label, and more. Installed via Helm (chart 2.5.25) and wired to the kube-prometheus-stack Prometheus.

setup_commands.command: helm repo add opencost https://opencost.github.io/opencost-helm-chart --force-update && helm repo update opencost
setup_commands.label: Adding OpenCost Helm repo

setup_commands.command: helm upgrade --install opencost opencost/opencost --version 2.5.25 --namespace opencost --create-namespace --set opencost.prometheus.internal.enabled=true --set opencost.prometheus.internal.serviceName=kube-prometheus-stack-prometheus --set opencost.prometheus.internal.namespaceName=monitoring --set opencost.prometheus.internal.port=9090 --wait --timeout 5m
setup_commands.label: Installing OpenCost

setup_commands.command: [ -f /var/run/opencost-pf.pid ] && kill $(cat /var/run/opencost-pf.pid) 2>/dev/null; sleep 1; nohup kubectl -n opencost port-forward --address 0.0.0.0 svc/opencost 9090:9090 9003:9003 >/var/log/opencost-portforward.log 2>&1 & echo $! >/var/run/opencost-pf.pid; sleep 2; echo 'OpenCost port-forward ready (UI :9090, API :9003)'
setup_commands.label: Starting OpenCost port-forward
